candidate: L8 POWER / SAMPLE-SIZE analysis -- quantify the L7 power-bottleneck. SYNTHESIS/meta track: NO new edge, NO new data. Reads the FROZEN L1 and L6 result JSONs, isolates the right-signed-but-underpowered LIQUID event-driven legs, and computes how many INDEPENDENT observations would be needed to reach |t|=2 (and 80% power), then compares to realistic BIST event-arrival rates.
purpose: L7 found that on LIQUID names the binding gate is SIGNIFICANCE/POWER (not cost): the surviving archetype (low-turnover, event-driven, right-signed) is correctly identified but has too few independent observations for |t|>=2. L8 converts 'we lack power' into a hard number: per-leg n_required for |t|=2 and for 80% power, the gap vs observed n, and whether realistic BIST event volumes for that event-class can ever reach it. Output sharpens FORWARD_DATA_SPEC priority (which event-class is power-reachable).
legs_analysed_FROZEN: L1 BIST30-add LIQUID post_[+1,+10] (date_clustered mean ~ +82bp, nw_t ~0.71, n_dates=12)
L1 BIST30-add LIQUID post_[+1,+5]  (date_clustered mean ~ +33bp, nw_t ~0.25, n_dates=12)

method_FROZEN.primary_relation: For a fixed effect size and per-unit variance the t-stat scales as sqrt(n): t = effect * sqrt(n). Hence n_required(target_t) = n_obs * (target_t / |t_obs|)^2, and gap_factor = (target_t / |t_obs|)^2. This needs NO sd reconstruction and is the robust headline.
method_FROZEN.implied_effect: Effective per-unit sd = |mean| * sqrt(n_obs) / |t_obs| (HAC-implied for L1 via nw_t; reproduces sd_car for L6 via clustered_t). Standardized effect d = |mean| / sd_unit, reported for transparency.

t_used_per_family.L1: nw_t (date-clustered HAC, the program keep-bar gate)

method_FROZEN.approximation_note: nw_t does NOT scale exactly as sqrt(n) (HAC adjustment depends on the autocorrelation structure that itself shifts with n); the sqrt(n) law is a first-order estimate, declared as such. The qualitative ranking (decades vs years) is robust to this.

event_rate_assumptions_FROZEN.note: Fixed a-priori arrival rates (NOT tuned). Used only to translate n_required into forward-years.

daily_pead_comparison.haircut_reason: earnings cluster in ~6-8 week seasons -> count distinct disclosure-DATES, not filings, to avoid overlap-inflated n (per L7 caution). Range 100-160; headline uses 120.

honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.summary: Expect the two SCARCE event classes (CPI 12/yr single-market; BIST30 reconstitution ~2 dates/yr) to require so many more independent observations that they are NOT reachable in a human-relevant horizon -- index-rebalance is essentially hopeless (decades), CPI-unconditional needs ~1.5-4x more events (~13-33 yr) so the real lever there is SURPRISE-CONDITIONING (sharpen d, not grow n). Only DAILY-PEAD has an event-arrival rate (~120 independent disclosure-dates/yr) large enough to reach plausible n_required within ~1-3 years. Net: L8 gives a NUMERIC ranking that confirms FORWARD_DATA_SPEC #1 (daily-PEAD) >> #2 (surprise-macro) >> index-rebalance-alone.
honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.most_likely_verdict: DESCRIPTIVE-POWER-VIEW. A decision tool, not an edge. NO celebration.
